title: Code Execution & General Agent Actions

actors.name: Agent
actors.description: AI agent driving all tool calls via GenericAgentHandler methods

actors.name: User
actors.description: Human who receives output, answers ask_user interrupts, and can trigger stop signals

actors.name: Browser (TMWebDriver)
actors.description: Headless/CDP-connected browser; accessed via driver.execute_js / driver.get_all_sessions

actors.name: File System
actors.description: Local disk; read and written by file_read / file_write / file_patch

actors.name: OS Process (subprocess)
actors.description: Child processes spawned by code_run for python/bash/powershell execution

use_cases.name: Execute Python Script
flow: Write code_run_header.py + user code to temp .ai.py file
Launch subprocess: python -X utf8 -u <tmp_path>
Stream stdout via background thread (stream_reader)
Kill on timeout or stop_signal
Cleanup temp file in finally block
Return {status, stdout, exit_code}

use_cases.name: Inline Eval (Python)
use_cases.note: <<extend>> of UC01 when inline_eval=True; runs eval/exec in-process with handler/parent in namespace

use_cases.name: Execute Shell Command
flow: Build cmd: ['bash','-c',code] on Unix or ['powershell','-Command',code] on Windows
Launch subprocess with STARTF_USESHOWWINDOW on Windows
Stream stdout; enforce timeout / stop_signal
Return {status, stdout, exit_code}

use_cases.name: Read File
flow: Resolve absolute path via _get_abs_path
file_read: stream lines with itertools; dropwhile < start
If keyword: scan with sliding deque window, return surrounding lines
Track _read_dirs for fuzzy file suggestions on FileNotFoundError
log_memory_access(path) to memory/file_access_stats.json if 'memory' in path
smart_format result to max 20000 chars
Return StepOutcome with next_prompt including WORKING MEMORY

use_cases.name: Write File
flow: Extract content from <file_content>...</file_content> tag or ```code block in response body
expand_file_refs: substitute {{file:path:start:end}} references with actual file slices
Write to file (open with 'w'/'a', or prepend by reading existing + rewriting)
Return {status:'success', writed_bytes:N}

use_cases.name: Patch File
flow: Resolve absolute path
expand_file_refs on new_content
file_patch: assert old_content appears exactly once
Replace old_content with new_content and write back
Error if 0 matches or >1 matches (with guidance)

use_cases.name: Scan Web Page
flow: Lazy-init TMWebDriver via first_init_driver()
Collect tabs metadata from driver.get_all_sessions()
Optionally switch active tab via driver.default_session_id
If not tabs_only: call simphtml.get_html(driver, cutlist=True, maxchars=35000)
Return metadata + HTML/text content

use_cases.name: Execute JavaScript in Browser
flow: Load script from arg, ```javascript block, or file path
Optionally switch active tab
Call simphtml.execute_js_rich(script, driver, no_monitor)
execute_js_rich: optionally capture baseline HTML + start temp text monitor
driver.execute_js(script) -> result
Detect new tabs opened during execution
If not no_monitor: collect transient texts, compute DOM diff via find_changed_elements
If save_to_file: write js_return to disk, truncate in-memory copy
Return {status, js_return, tab_id, transients, diff, newTabs}

use_cases.name: Save JS Result to File
use_cases.note: <<extend>> of UC07 when save_to_file arg provided

use_cases.name: Ask User for Input
flow: Return StepOutcome with status=INTERRUPT, intent=HUMAN_INTERVENTION
should_exit=True — halts agent loop until user replies
use_cases.actor_response: User provides answer; agent loop resumes

use_cases.name: Prepend Code Run Header
use_cases.note: <<include>> of UC01 — assets/code_run_header.py injected before user Python code; patches subprocess.run for encoding, installs ImportError/AttributeError hint hook

use_cases.name: Stream Subprocess Output
use_cases.note: <<include>> of UC01/UC02 — background daemon thread reads stdout lines; main thread polls for timeout/stop_signal every 1s

use_cases.name: Optimise HTML for Tokens
use_cases.note: <<include>> of UC06 — optimize_html_for_tokens strips style attrs, truncates long src/href, removes non-semantic attributes; uses BeautifulSoup

use_cases.name: Cut Repetitive List Items
use_cases.note: <<include>> of UC06 when cutlist=True — js_findMainList scores candidate containers, keeps 3 representative items, inserts [FAKE ELEMENT] hint; smart_truncate reduces to maxchars

use_cases.name: Monitor DOM Changes
use_cases.note: <<include>> of UC07 unless no_monitor=True — captures baseline HTML + installs interval text monitor (450ms tick); after execution computes diff via find_changed_elements and collects transient strings

use_cases.name: Update Working Checkpoint
flow: Persist key_info / related_sop in self.working dict
Injected into next_prompt via _get_anchor_prompt as <key_info> block

use_cases.name: Trigger Long-Term Memory Update
flow: Read memory/memory_management_sop.md
Return detailed prompt instructing agent to file_patch L1/L2/L3 memory files
Agent subsequently calls file_read + file_patch to commit distilled facts

key_relationships: GenericAgentHandler aggregates TMWebDriver via module-level driver singleton
GenericAgentHandler delegates to module-level functions: code_run, web_scan, web_execute_js, file_read, file_patch, ask_user
simphtml.get_html calls get_main_block (js_optHTML) + js_findMainList + optimize_html_for_tokens + smart_truncate
simphtml.execute_js_rich calls get_html, start_temp_monitor, driver.execute_js, get_temp_texts, find_changed_elements
code_run (python mode) includes assets/code_run_header.py as script preamble
file_write / file_patch both call expand_file_refs for {{file:}} reference expansion
important_notes: code_run streams output in real-time; temp .ai.py files cleaned up in finally
Timeout default=60s; stop_signal list allows external kill
web_scan/web_execute_js lazy-init driver; require at least one open browser tab
simphtml js_optHTML runs entirely in-browser JS, filters hidden/overlay elements, shadow DOM, iframes
execute_js_rich returns transients (appeared-then-disappeared text), DOM diff, and newTabs detection
smart_format caps output length before returning to LLM context
Memory access logging tracks read frequency per file in memory/file_access_stats.json
turn_end_callback injects turn-count warnings and plan-mode hints into next_prompt every N turns
